name: JBSA Lackland
tagline: Gateway to the Air Force: home of Air Force & Space Force Basic Military Training.
_note: Base #2 (schema-portability proof). Facts verified from official sources (jbsa.mil, 37trw.af.mil, basictraining.af.mil, wilfordhall.tricare.mil, lacklandisd.net, nisd.net, DeCA/AAFES, DoD BAH tool). BMT-graduation killer content is general + links the official site; detailed week-of logistics + curated local listings to come from the Touch Stay content. Housing partner (Balfour Beatty), base coords, and off-base ZIP are medium-confidence (confirm before heavy reliance).

data.Branch: U.S. Air Force (Joint Base San Antonio)
data.Mission: Air Force & Space Force Basic Military Training (BMT)

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: JBSA-Lackland is home to the 37th Training Wing, the largest training wing in the Department of the Air Force and known as the 'Gateway to the Air Force.'

Its 737th Training Group runs all enlisted Basic Military Training (BMT) for both the Air Force and Space Force, tens of thousands of recruits a year, alongside a wide range of technical and professional training. JBSA itself is the Department of Defense's largest joint base.

data.subtitle: Lackland is the only Basic Military Training site for the Air Force and Space Force, so for most families the reason you're here is graduation week.
intro: Graduation week is when you'll see your Airman or Guardian for the first time since they shipped out. Families travel to San Antonio from across the country for it, and a little planning makes the trip far less stressful.

Graduation is held weekly (typically Thursdays) and includes the Airman's Run. Schedules, locations, dress expectations, and base-access rules change week to week, so always confirm the current details on the official BMT graduation site before you travel.

recommendations.advice: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID. Check the JBSA-Lackland visitor info page for current gate and pass requirements.
Graduation weeks fill up fast. On-base lodging is limited, but there are plenty of hotels near the base and across San Antonio.

recommendations.who: Make a long weekend of it
recommendations.advice: Most families do. The River Walk, the Alamo, and San Antonio's food scene are all close by.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: JBSA-Lackland controls access at its gates; visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass plus a REAL ID-compliant ID.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official JBSA-Lackland visitor information page before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: On-base family housing at JBSA-Lackland is privatized, operated by Balfour Beatty Communities.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so check the Air Force Housing office and the housing partner for current options and waitlists.

source.label: DoD BAH Rate Lookup (MHA TX285, CY2026)

sections.title: Schools
data: On base, schooling is the civilian Lackland Independent School District (Lackland Elementary and Virginia Allred Stacey Jr/Sr High School). There is no DoDEA district here.
Off base, the area northwest of the installation is served largely by Northside Independent School District, one of the largest districts in Texas.

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the answer is mostly about how long you'll be here and how steady the local market is.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or your orders feel uncertain.
You want time to learn the area before committing to a neighborhood.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The market is steady and you could rent the home out later if needed.
You've found a neighborhood and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length and the local market, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the housing office's read on off-base neighborhoods before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining school district or DoDEA early and bring records and immunizations. The School Liaison Office can help with the transfer.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE region and enrollment so you can be seen at the local military treatment facility or network providers.

data.q: Why am I coming to Lackland?
data.a: Lackland runs Air Force and Space Force Basic Military Training, so for many families the visit is graduation week. It is also home to the 37th Training Wing and a great deal of technical training.

data.q: How do I attend a BMT graduation?
data.a: Graduation is typically held weekly. Bring a REAL ID and arrange base access in advance, and check the official BMT graduation site for the current schedule and rules before you travel.

data.q: On base or off base in San Antonio?
data.a: On-base family housing is run by Balfour Beatty. Off base, families spread across San Antonio, so your commute and schools depend on where you land.
All of the JBSA bases share MHA TX285 (San Antonio). Use the calculator above for your grade.

sections.title: Food & Things Nearby
sections.note: Live nearby places via Google Places. Tap 'Use my location' to re-center on where you are.

picks.name: The Alamo
picks.blurb: The iconic 1718 mission and battle site in the heart of downtown San Antonio. Free to enter.

picks.name: San Antonio River Walk
picks.blurb: The city's signature network of riverside walkways, restaurants, and boat tours, just below street level downtown.

picks.name: The Pearl
picks.blurb: A former brewery turned dining and shopping district north of downtown, with a popular weekend farmers market.

picks.name: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
picks.blurb: Four Spanish colonial missions preserved by the National Park Service along the river, part of Texas's only UNESCO World Heritage Site.
picks.maps_query: San Antonio Missions National Historical Park
